steps.title: Spot the needed word type
steps.content: After the verb landed we need a preposition to introduce the location noun phrase Edwards Air Force Base. No punctuation should come between that preposition and its object.

steps.title: Apply the preposition rule
steps.content:
Choice D gives just the word at with no extra mark, correctly linking the verb to its object.
The other choices add an em dash, a comma, or a colon, which would incorrectly separate the preposition from the noun phrase and violate the rule that a preposition takes its object directly.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
